description: Chinese user-editable encyclopedia, founded 2005 as a Baidu Baike rival. ByteDance bought it in 2019 and rebranded it Douyin Baike.
ByteDance's online doctor-consult and health app. Never shut, but in 2023 its super-app plan faded and the team moved to Douyin's medical arm.
ByteDance's online insurance marketplace in Douyin and Toutiao. The 头号保 brand faded around 2021; ByteDance kept the broker license.
Crystal of Atlan: a free anime action MMO and ByteDance's first homegrown mobile hit (2023). It survived the gaming retreat and still runs.
Sci-fi survival RPG set on a ruined future Earth, and Nuverse's flagship. When ByteDance quit gaming it sold the studio to Tencent; the game runs on.
Dice-driven open-world RPG with a Dungeons & Dragons crossover, from ByteDance's Nuverse. US servers closed in 2024 as ByteDance quit gaming; Tencent took the IP.
Chinese-style otome game from ByteDance's Nuverse, mixing cat-raising with five suitors. After losses Nuverse passed it to the developer; it closed on 2023-12-31.
Wuxia mobile RPG from ByteDance's Nuverse, from the comic Blades of the Guardians. It topped China's iOS free chart on launch in 2021, then closed a year later.
Mobile battle royale built by NetEase, licensed from Terminator 2. ByteDance's Nuverse ran the mainland version from 2020 until servers closed on 2021-12-29.

original_name: 战争艺术：赤潮 (Art of War: Red Tides)
description: Multiplayer strategy game from Game Science, later of Black Myth: Wukong, published in China by ByteDance's Nuverse. Its mainland servers closed on 2022-05-31.
DC superhero mobile RPG from ByteDance's Nuverse. Soft-launched only in Southeast Asia, it closed about five months later and never went global.
Warhammer 40,000 strategy mobile game from ByteDance's Nuverse. Its global servers closed in February 2024 when ByteDance wound down Nuverse.
Nuverse's Southeast Asia mobile remake of Ragnarok Online. ByteDance marked it for sale in 2023 but no deal closed; it survived and became Ragnarok X: Together.
Chinese smartphone line, born as Luo Yonghao's Smartisan. ByteDance hired the team in 2019 and shipped Douyin phones, then killed it in January 2021 after weak sales.
ByteDance's planned next VR headset, to follow the Pico 4. After weak Pico 4 sales it quit the Meta Quest fight and scrapped the Pico 5 in 2023; it never shipped.
VR headset maker founded 2015, bought by ByteDance in 2021 to fight Meta's Quest. Cut back in 2023 but still going, with the PICO 4 Ultra and 2026 Project Swan.
Global women's fast-fashion site, widely tied to ByteDance and built to fight SHEIN. It closed abruptly about three months after launch, having won few users.
ByteDance's shopping app selling cheap factory-direct goods to Europe. Closed in 2023 over weak repeat sales, poor quality and slow shipping.
ByteDance's first short-video app, aimed at China's smaller cities with livestreaming and cash rewards. Folded into Douyin in 2020 and renamed Douyin Huoshan Edition.
ByteDance's overseas AI chatbot, sibling to China's Doubao, on OpenAI and Google models. Renamed Dola in 2025; the app runs on under the new name.
ByteDance's overseas AI fiction and character-chat app, launched 2023 on OpenAI's GPT. Renamed AnyDoor in 2024; the mobile apps were later pulled from the stores.
ByteDance's China-only flagship short-video app, sibling to TikTok. Launched 2016 as A.me, now China's top short-video and social-commerce app. Still alive.
Toutiao: ByteDance's first flagship, a 2012 app that builds personalized news feeds by machine learning. Still running in 2026 with over 300M monthly users.
ByteDance's mid-length video app, once pitched as China's YouTube. Launched 2016 as Toutiao Video, renamed Xigua in 2017; still live, folded into Douyin since 2024.
Faceu: an AR selfie camera for iOS and Android, with live stickers, face effects and filters. ByteDance bought it in 2018; still maintained.
Jianying: ByteDance's main China video editor and Douyin's creation tool, with AI subtitles, voiceover and cutout. Sibling to CapCut; still maintained.
Xingtu: ByteDance's China photo and AI image editor, launched 2020 as the still-image partner to Jianying. Still alive, with over 60M monthly users.
ByteDance's beauty selfie camera (China: Qingyan; overseas: Ulike), with live face touch-up, AI makeup and filters. Built by the Faceu team in 2018; still alive.
ByteDance's AI image and video app from the Jianying team, on the in-house Seedream and Seedance models. Called Jimeng in China, Dreamina overseas. Still alive.
ByteDance's overseas AI marketing app, built on CapCut, making product videos, images and AI avatars for online sellers. Renamed from CapCut Commerce Pro in 2025; still alive.
TikTok's music distribution and marketing tool for new artists, with no upfront fees and full royalties on ByteDance apps. Still alive; not the shut TikTok Music app.
Soda Music: ByteDance's algorithm-driven, Douyin-linked music app for China. Still growing: about 156M monthly users by March 2026, China's third-largest music app.
Doubao: ByteDance's main consumer AI assistant, launched August 2023 on its own model. China's most-used AI-native app, over 330M monthly users in 2026. Still alive.
Coze: ByteDance's no-code AI agent and chatbot builder, its take on OpenAI's GPTs. Still alive and growing: open-sourced in 2025, v3.0 in 2026.
ChitChop: ByteDance's overseas AI-assistant app, twin of China's Little Wukong, with 200-plus bots for work, study and creation. Quietly shut down in 2024.
Trae: ByteDance's AI code editor, a fork of VS Code with a built-in agent for chat-driven coding. Launched January 2025; still developed and growing.
ByteDance's AI video model, from its Make Pixels Dance paper, launched under the Doubao brand for multi-shot clips. Folded into the Seedance model line in 2025.
ByteDance's foundation-model team (2023) and the brand over its Doubao LLMs, Seed-OSS open weights, Seedream image and Seedance video models. Still active.
Feishu: ByteDance's all-in-one work app for China, sibling to the overseas Lark, bundling chat, docs, video meetings, calendar, workflows and AI agents. Still alive.
ByteDance's enterprise cloud, big-data and AI platform, launched 2020 and now the main channel for its Doubao models. Still alive: China's top enterprise AI cloud.
ByteDance's overseas B2B arm, selling the recommendation, AR-effects and AI tech behind TikTok via cloud APIs. Launched March 2021; still alive and expanding.
ByteDance's mobile ad network and SDK, the ad arm of TikTok for Business. The overseas brand of China's Chuanshanjia; still alive and now live in the US.
ByteDance's free, ad-supported web-fiction app, also audiobooks and short dramas. Still alive: China's top reading app, 100M-plus daily users in 2025.
ByteDance's long-audio app, a 2020 spinoff of Fanqie Novel that used AI text-to-speech over voice actors. Still alive; it passed Ximalaya in users in late 2024.
ByteDance's free, ad-supported micro-drama app, launched May 2023, that undercut China's pay-per-episode model. Still growing: 304M monthly users by February 2026.
In-app payment service in Douyin, launched January 2021 to rival Alipay and WeChat Pay. Built on a license from the acquired firm Ulpay. Still active.
TikTok's in-app shopping marketplace, with a Shop tab, livestream selling and an affiliate program. Still alive; banned in Indonesia in 2023, then merged into Tokopedia.
ByteDance's Chinese live-commerce business in Douyin: livestream selling, product links and the Douyin Mall storefront. Still growing: about 3.5 trillion RMB in 2024 sales.
Dongchedi: a leading Chinese car-info and car-shopping app that began as the auto channel of ByteDance's Toutiao. Spun out of ByteDance, eyeing a 2026 Hong Kong listing.

original_name: 航海王热血航线 (One Piece: Fighting Path)
description: Licensed One Piece 3D action-RPG for mobile, built by CMGE and published only by ByteDance's Nuverse. A launch-day hit; it survived the 2023 retreat and still runs.

original_name: 花亦山心之月 (The Moonlit Oath)
description: Nuverse's first self-made game, a Chinese-style gacha card RPG of court intrigue at a royal academy. It survived the 2023-24 Nuverse wind-down and still runs in China.
A short-lived ByteDance hardware product, discontinued in early 2019. Reported by Vogue China; the launch date and details are unconfirmed.
China's top photography community and stock-image site, and ByteDance's first-ever buy (December 2014). ByteDance sold its whole stake to Jishi Design in October 2024.
